evals.prompt: My storefront is showing $0.00 for all product prices. Everything else looks fine — products are loading, search works. Where do I start debugging?
evals.expected_output: Should narrow down where prices come from in an EDS + ACO setup: the Catalog Service resolves prices from the Price Book associated with the active catalog view. Diagnostic steps should cover at least 2 to 3 of: (a) inspect the Catalog Service request in DevTools Network tab to see whether prices are zero in the JSON response itself vs. just in the rendered UI; (b) verify Storefront Configuration headers.cs contains a valid AC-View-ID; (c) confirm in ACO admin that the catalog view has an associated Price Book and that the Price Book is populated; (d) check that price data has synced from Adobe Commerce to ACO via the Optimizer Connector or SaaS data export. Should not invent header names — any header named should match the documented names from the Adobe Commerce storefront configuration documentation.

evals.prompt: I'm setting up a brand new EDS storefront. I've forked the boilerplate and connected it to my DA site. What do I need to configure to get it talking to my ACO backend?
evals.expected_output: Should cover the documented setup for an EDS storefront talking to ACO: (1) the Storefront Configuration file is `config.json` in the repo (taking precedence when present) and is also managed at the EDS Config Service at `admin.hlx.page/config/{ORG}/sites/{SITE}/public.json` for production — the docs warn not to commit `config.json` to `main` for production sites. (2) Minimum documented config keys for ACO: `commerce-endpoint` (ACO GraphQL endpoint), `headers.cs.AC-View-ID` (catalog view ID, required), `analytics.environment-id` (tenant ID), `analytics.view-id`, and `analytics.base-currency-code`. `headers.cs.AC-Price-Book-ID` is documented as optional. (3) The configuration is cached by the EDS CDN with `max-age=7200` (2 hours), so changes take up to 2 hours to propagate. Should not invent config keys — any key cited should match documented names.

evals.prompt: I'm getting a 418 Configuration Error page when I load my storefront. The page just says 'Configuration Error'. How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Should walk through documented diagnostics for a Configuration Error on storefront load: (1) Clear stale config — the configuration is cached in browser `sessionStorage` and on the EDS CDN (`max-age=7200`, 2 hours); use DevTools > Application > Session Storage to clear, or test in an incognito window. (2) Validate the Storefront Configuration JSON is well-formed and contains the required keys for the active backend. (3) Verify the configured endpoints (e.g. `commerce-endpoint`) are reachable — a direct curl against the endpoint distinguishes a network/CORS failure from a config-parsing failure. (4) Inspect the browser console — startup errors from the storefront's initialization scripts surface there. Should not assert specific internal causes (e.g. "418 means X function threw") unless documented.

evals.prompt: I want to add a custom warranty_info field from my product catalog to the PDP. The attribute exists in Adobe Commerce. How do I get it to show up in the drop-in?
evals.expected_output: Should explain overrideGQLOperations in build.mjs as the correct approach — add the field to the ProductDetailsFragment. Should note prerequisites: 'Use in GraphQL' must be enabled on the attribute in Adobe Commerce admin, and if using ACO/Catalog Service it must also be synced via SaaS data export. Should explain that after modifying build.mjs, npm install must be re-run.

evals.prompt: I pushed a change to my storefront repo but the update isn't showing on my aem.live URL. It's been 10 minutes. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Should cover: (1) Verify AEM Code Sync GitHub App is installed on the repo. (2) File paths on CDN are case-sensitive. (3) Check sync status via AEM Admin API. (4) Force re-sync options: Admin API POST, trivial commit to trigger webhook, or PR checkbox. Should reference aem.live docs for Admin API authentication details.

evals.prompt: I'm using the EDS boilerplate storefront with the Commerce drop-in components. How do I add a product recommendations unit to a category page? It's not a PDP so I'm not sure how to pass the context.
evals.expected_output: Should explain the DA block table structure for adding a product-recommendations block: block name in the first cell, recId row with the recommendation unit ID from ACO, optional currentSku row for context-aware types. Should clarify that currentSku is only needed on non-PDP pages when using context-aware types (e.g. Customers Also Viewed) — on a PDP the block auto-detects SKU from adobeDataLayer. Must NOT answer using a headless JS SDK approach (fabricating npm packages, SDK client classes, or .register()/.fetch()/.render() methods) — the correct EDS approach is the DA block + drop-in component, not a standalone JS SDK integration. Should fetch from the Storefront documentation for current block format and drop-in API details.

evals.prompt: How do I customize the layout of the cart drop-in? I want to move the order summary to the left side.
evals.expected_output: Should explain that the documented way to customize drop-in layout is the granular containers approach — rendering individual containers into a custom DOM structure rather than relying on the default monolithic container. A CSS ordering approach (using 'order' on wrapper columns) is also acceptable if the class names are cited from the boilerplate or fetched documentation, but the response must still mention the containers API as the primary supported customization surface. Must warn not to modify files in scripts/__dropins__/ directly — that directory is closed-source and will be overwritten on npm install. Must not invent container names or CSS class names — any specific names must come from fetched Storefront documentation or the boilerplate reference repo.

evals.prompt: What is the difference between commerce-endpoint and commerce-core-endpoint in Storefront Configuration? Do I need both?
evals.expected_output: Should clearly explain the two-endpoint split: commerce-endpoint is always ACO (Catalog Service) — handles search, PDP, recommendations, pricing. commerce-core-endpoint is Adobe Commerce (transactions) — handles cart, checkout, auth, B2B. Should note that commerce-core-endpoint is optional: if omitted, both fall back to commerce-endpoint, which means ACO handles everything (only valid if ACO supports those operations). Should reference the Storefront Configuration documentation for the full config key list.

evals.prompt: How do I create a new custom block for my EDS storefront? I want to build a featured-products block that shows a hardcoded list of SKUs.
evals.expected_output: Should explain the block structure: blocks/<name>/<name>.js exporting a default decorate(block) function, optional <name>.css. Should explain how to read block config from DA using readBlockConfig. Should note blocks are triggered by EDS based on section markup in DA. Should advise checking the Storefront documentation and the user's existing blocks/ directory for current block patterns and repo conventions.

evals.prompt: I've just cloned the storefront boilerplate repo and want to run it locally. What do I need to install and what command starts the local dev server?
evals.expected_output: Should cover the documented local dev setup: (1) run `npm install` to install project dependencies including drop-in packages into scripts/__dropins__/; (2) install the AEM CLI globally (npm install -g @adobe/aem-cli or equivalent); (3) run `aem up` to start the local proxy server which proxies content from the connected aem.page preview origin. Should explain that the local dev server proxies content (DA pages, Sidekick-previewed content) from the EDS CDN — it does not run a local CMS. Should note that changes to JS/CSS files are served locally while content comes from the connected DA site. Should fetch from the AEM/EDS developer docs and/or the boilerplate README before naming specific CLI commands or package names. Must not invent command flags or package scopes.

evals.prompt: I'm getting CORS errors in the browser console when my storefront tries to call my Adobe Commerce backend. The error references my .aem.live domain being blocked. How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Should clearly explain that CORS must be configured on the Adobe Commerce backend side — the EDS/storefront side has no CORS to configure. Should explain that the Commerce backend needs to allow origins including *.aem.live, *.aem.page, and the production custom domain. Should distinguish configuration paths for ACCS vs PaaS: PaaS configuration is typically in .htaccess or Nginx config depending on the cloud tier, while ACCS may have different admin-surface config. Should note that CORS failures on authentication requests have downstream effects — they can manifest as $0 prices, guest-only checkout, or personalization not working because auth tokens cannot be obtained. Should fetch from both Storefront troubleshooting docs and ExL Commerce docs for the backend CORS configuration steps. Must not invent specific config directives or Admin paths — fetch documentation to confirm.

evals.prompt: I've enabled B2B features on my Adobe Commerce backend — company accounts and purchase orders are working in the Admin. But on my EDS storefront, company login and the purchase order UI aren't showing up. What Storefront Configuration do I need to set?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that the EDS storefront has Storefront Configuration flags that gate B2B UI features in the drop-ins, separate from the backend module being enabled. Should cover: the B2B features must be explicitly enabled in Storefront Configuration with the documented config keys, and both the backend (B2B module enabled) and the storefront config flags must be set for the features to appear. Should fetch from the Storefront Configuration documentation before naming any specific config key names — must not invent flag names such as 'commerce-b2b-enabled' without confirming them from the docs. Should note that some B2B features also require the commerce-core-endpoint to be configured (pointing to the ACCS/PaaS instance, not just ACO).

evals.prompt: How do I add login and account features to my EDS storefront? I see there's a storefront-auth drop-in but I'm not sure how to set it up.
evals.expected_output: Should explain the storefront-auth drop-in installation and initialization pattern: (1) the drop-in is installed as an npm package and wired via the import map in head.html; (2) an initializer file in scripts/initializers/ configures the drop-in with the Commerce customer endpoint and any custom event handlers; (3) the auth drop-in provides sign-in, sign-up, forgot-password, and token management, and integrates with the commerce-core-endpoint for customer authentication. Should note that the auth drop-in is a prerequisite for account, order history, and B2B features. Should fetch from the Storefront drop-in documentation before naming specific initializer config keys, event names, or import alias values — must not invent these. Should advise reading scripts/initializers/ in the user's repo if available to see how other drop-ins are initialized as a reference pattern.

evals.prompt: My checkout drop-in just doesn't render — the page loads but the drop-in area is completely blank. There's no 418 Configuration Error and no obvious JavaScript errors in the console. Where do I look?
evals.expected_output: Should distinguish this from the 418 Configuration Error scenario (which has an explicit error page). Diagnostic steps should cover at least 3 of: (a) verify the import map in head.html has a correct alias for the checkout drop-in package pointing to the installed version in scripts/__dropins__/ — an incorrect alias means the module silently fails to load; (b) verify npm install was run and scripts/__dropins__/storefront-checkout/ (or equivalent) is populated with the drop-in source; (c) check that the block name in the DA page matches the expected block name exactly (EDS block lookup is case-sensitive and hyphen-normalized); (d) check that AEM Code Sync has deployed the latest code changes — the Admin API status endpoint shows whether the CDN copy is current; (e) check the browser Network tab for 404s on drop-in module fetches. Should fetch from the Storefront documentation and/or the boilerplate reference repo (hlxsites/aem-boilerplate-commerce) to confirm import map syntax and block naming conventions before recommending specific values. Must not invent module alias names or import map syntax.
